# terrible_purestorage_flasharray_purefa_pgsched (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the protection group.
- `schedule` (String) Which schedule to change.

### Optional

- `all_for` (Number) Specifies the length of time, in seconds, to keep the snapshots on the source array before they are eradicated. Range available 1 - 34560000.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `blackout_end` (String) Specifies the time at which to restart replication. Provide a time in 12-hour AM/PM format, eg. 5PM
- `blackout_start` (String) Specifies the time at which to suspend replication. Provide a time in 12-hour AM/PM format, eg. 11AM
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `context` (String) Name of fleet member on which to perform the operation. This requires the array receiving the request is a member of a fleet and the context name to be a member of the same fleet.
- `days` (Number) Specifies the number of days to keep the *per_day* snapshots beyond the *all_for* period before they are eradicated Max retention period is 4000 days
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable the schedule being configured.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `per_day` (Number) Specifies the number of *per_day* snapshots to keep beyond the *all_for* period. Maximum number is 1440
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `replicate_at` (String) Provide a time in 12-hour AM/PM format, eg. 11AM Only valid if *replicate_frequency* is an exact multiple of 86400, ie 1 day.
- `replicate_frequency` (Number) Specifies the replication frequency in seconds. Range 900 - 34560000 (FA-405, //M10, //X10 and Cloud Block Store). Range 300 - 34560000 (all other arrays).
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `snap_at` (String) Provide a time in 12-hour AM/PM format, eg. 11AM Only valid if *snap_frequency* is an exact multiple of 86400, ie 1 day.
- `snap_frequency` (Number) Specifies the snapshot frequency in seconds. Range available 300 - 34560000.
- `state` (String) Define whether to set or delete the protection group schedule.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `target_all_for` (Number) Specifies the length of time, in seconds, to keep the replicated snapshots on the targets. Range is 1 - 34560000 seconds.
- `target_days` (Number) Specifies the number of days to keep the *target_per_day* replicated snapshots beyond the *target_all_for* period before they are eradicated. Max retention period is 4000 days
- `target_per_day` (Number) Specifies the number of *per_day* replicated snapshots to keep beyond the *target_all_for* period. Maximum number is 1440
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
